pending.body: src/adapters/worktree.rs:155-185 force-removes every `assume-unchanged` path from the checkpoint index, and tests/unit/checkpoint_test.rs:383-418 codifies that behavior. These files are still tracked, so removing them from the tree turns them into deletions instead of preserving the parent-tree version. In a simple git repro, the resulting checkpoint cannot be restored: `git reset --hard <checkpoint>` fails with `Entry 'assume.txt' not uptodate. Cannot merge.` once the checkpoint commit has dropped the file. This breaks the safety contract of checkpoints for any repo that uses `assume-unchanged` locally.

src/adapters/worktree.rs:137-152 queries ignored paths with `git ls-files -i -c --exclude-standard`, then src/adapters/worktree.rs:173-185 removes every match from the checkpoint tree. `--exclude-standard` also honors `.git/info/exclude` and the developer's global `core.excludesFile`, so checkpoint contents now depend on user-local git configuration rather than repository state. A local global-ignore rule matching a tracked file is enough to make that file disappear from checkpoint commits entirely. That is nondeterministic and can silently corrupt checkpoints on one machine but not another.

Remove the stray `prompt-t7j.md` file from the repo root before merging. It was added at the root by the first checkpoint commit (7518cc15) via `git add -A -- .`, is byte-identical to `.ralph-burning/projects/t7j-1/prompt.md`, and is not covered by `.gitignore`. It is therefore not excluded by the Part A fix and would land on master if this branch is merged, polluting the top-level tree with a transient scratchpad duplicate of the bead prompt. Recommended remediation: `git rm prompt-t7j.md && git commit -m "chore: remove stray bead prompt scratchpad from repo root"`. This is directly adjacent to the bead's stated acceptance goal of keeping master clean.
